ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class (FOODON_03302051). No specific entry for spicy Mexican-style marinated chicken wings exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers cooked chicken wings generically. No Mexican-spiced variant exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for spicy Mexican chicken wings. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual prepared poultry dishes at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 chicken wings, estimated at approximately 480 g total, consistent with restaurant portion sizes for bone-in wings at approximately 80 g per wing.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, cooked, NS as to cooking method — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05192)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy data for cooked chicken wings (NDB 05192 / FDC 171477) used as the primary nutrient base. Values represent cooked bone-in chicken wing flesh. Adjustment applied to reflect spiced Mexican marinade contribution: sodium elevated by approximately 80 mg/100 g to account for salt, chilli, and seasoning blend typical of Mexican-style preparations. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values retained from USDA cooked chicken wing baseline. This is an adapted estimate; exact restaurant formulation is not publicly available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Chicken wings contain negligible carbohydrate (0 g/100 g per USDA data). Pure protein-and-fat foods have a glycemic index of effectively 0 and do not raise blood glucose meaningfully. Per the University of Sydney GI Database methodology, foods with no available carbohydrate are assigned GI = 0 (not testable). For database conformity requiring a numeric GI > 0, GI is set to 1 (lowest valid positive sentinel) with gi_category = low. Glycemic load per serving = 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. This is consistent with peer-reviewed GI literature for high-protein, zero-carbohydrate meat dishes.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium from seasoning and marinade
hypertension.guidance: Each 6-piece serving (480 g) delivers approximately 1,584 mg sodium, representing 69–79% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid additional high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content with high protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Spicy Mexican chicken wings contain effectively zero carbohydrate and have a glycemic index of 1, making them unlikely to raise postprandial blood glucose; however, the high fat content warrants attention to overall caloric intake for weight management in type 2 diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol content from skin-on chicken wings
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 6-piece serving provides approximately 22.6 g saturated fat and 461 mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption frequency and consider removing the skin to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Mexican Chicken Wings (6 pieces) are bone-in chicken wings marinated in a bold Mexican-inspired spice blend typically comprising chilli peppers, cumin, paprika, garlic, oregano, and lime juice, then fried or baked until the skin is crispy and the flesh is juicy. The dish delivers an intensely spicy, smoky, and tangy flavour profile characteristic of Mexican cuisine, with a crispy exterior giving way to tender, well-seasoned meat. In Singapore, this dish is served at Mexican-themed restaurants and fusion eateries, often as a starter or sharing plate. Nutritionally, the wings are high in protein (approximately 27 g/100 g) and fat (approximately 17 g/100 g), with negligible carbohydrate content, making them suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. The sodium content is elevated due to the seasoning marinade, which is a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The saturated fat from the chicken skin is relevant for cardiovascular health. The glycemic index is effectively zero (set to 1 for database conformity), posing minimal glycemic risk for individuals with type 2 diabetes, though caloric density should be monitored. A standard restaurant serving of 6 wings weighs approximately 480 g and provides around 1,277 kcal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 6 Mexican chicken wings?
Are spicy Mexican wings high in protein?
Can diabetics eat Mexican chicken wings?
Is Mexican chicken wings high in sodium?
What is the GI of chicken wings?
How much fat in Mexican chicken wings?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken wing data (FDC 171477), which is an approved primary source for generic Western poultry dishes. The Mexican spice marinade contribution to sodium has been estimated. No exact nutritional data for this specific restaurant product is publicly available from an approved source. GI is an analogous estimate based on the zero-carbohydrate nature of the food, consistent with peer-reviewed GI literature.
notes: 1. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain cooked chicken wings; the Mexican spice marinade adds negligible carbohydrate. This is a confirmed zero value for the base ingredient. 2. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain cooked chicken wings; marinade may add trace sugar but below detection threshold — treated as trace. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked chicken wings; naturally zero for a meat product with no plant-based components. 4. glycemic_index is set to 1 (lowest valid positive sentinel) as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g is 0. This is consistent with peer-reviewed GI literature for high-protein, zero-carbohydrate meat dishes. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for cooked chicken wings. 6. Sodium value of 330 mg/100 g is an adapted estimate: USDA baseline for cooked chicken wings (~70 mg/100 g) plus approximately 260 mg/100 g adjustment for Mexican spice marinade salt contribution, consistent with seasoned restaurant-style preparations. 7. Halal status is listed as false as it cannot be confirmed from public sources for these specific restaurants; consumers should verify directly with the restaurant. 8. The serving size of 480 g (6 × 80 g per wing) is an estimate based on typical bone-in chicken wing weights; actual restaurant portion may vary. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all sourced from USDA FoodData Central FDC 171477 for cooked chicken wings.
